result:
Fix is in place and builds clean. Summary:

## Root cause
`crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2501` in `cg_indexed`: when generating an indexed-by-string access (e.g. `math.ult`), the code unconditionally classified the access as `IndexStr` (ã `OP_GETFIELD`). `OP_GETFIELD` packs the key's constant index into an 8-bit `C` field. The function had already computed `k_is_kstr` (which gates on `k.u.info <= MAXARG_B` = 255), but it only consulted that flag for the UpVal branch, not the local/non-reloc branch. In `math.lua`, by the time we reached line 658 (`math.ult(3, 4)`), the chunk's constants table held "ult" at index **258**. The emitter then truncated 258 to 2 in the C field, and `OP_GETFIELD R13, R13, k[2]` looked up `math._nomsg` (nil) instead of `math.ult`, producing "attempt to call a nil value".

## Fix
`crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2501` ã changed `if k.k == ExprKind::K {` to `if k.k == ExprKind::K && k_is_kstr {`. When the string-constant index doesn't fit in 8 bits, control now falls through to the `Indexed` branch (key loaded into a register, `OP_GETTABLE` emitted) ã matching Lua 5.4's `luaK_indexed` (`isKstr` gates `VINDEXSTR` on `info <= MAXARG_B`).

## Post-fix state
`cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test per the loop's instructions; the mega-loop will re-verify.
